blocks.content: Three seconds. Five. Long enough for it to become a thing.
A sophomore bumped his shoulder going past and didn't apologize. He stepped to the right. Ended up at a table near the back — not by design, just by drift. Sat down. Set his tray down.
Nobody said anything to him. He didn't say anything to anyone.
At the athlete table Jonathan was mid-story, hands moving, the whole table leaning in. Maya Reeves glanced over from near the windows, then back to her own conversation.
Both of them had seen him standing there.
He ate his lunch. He watched people have things figured out. He tried to remember when that had ever been easy.

blocks.text: is going to have to figure something out.
